stemHtml: You are the pilot flying of an aircraft with normal speed, and the load factor stable and constant. While climbing, you feel vibrations through the fuselage and, in particular, the rudder pedals. In response, you apply a very small and gentle pedal input, which stops the vibration. This vibration is a result of...

options.a: Slack in the control linkages, which, combined with the current speed, causes the rudder to vibrate.
options.b: Elasticity, and if the pilots fly at a faster speed, it will go away.
options.c: Elasticity, which is caused by the relative flexible construction of, in particular, the wings.
options.d: Flutter and, if the speed is maintained, the unstable vibration will increase and the rudder will disintegrate.
